note: Adding backup=True/backup_suffix params to Image.save() in Image.py; need to modify the save() method signature and body to copy existing file before overwrite. Tests added to Tests/test_image.py for backup functionality.

type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run param to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py (~line 2469-2591); using an in-memory/null file target instead of opening the real file when dry_run=True, and skipping fp.close()/file creation when dry_run. Also adding tests to Tests/test_image.py (likely a new test function e.g. test_dry_run or similar, near existing save-related tests).

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: I will own src/PIL/Image.py and implement both dry_run (mine) and backup/backup_suffix (yours) in the save() method. You own Tests/test_image.py and will implement tests for both features.

type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image.py
spec: Please add tests for the dry_run feature in Image.save(). Spec: Image.save(fp, format=None, dry_run=False, **params) — when dry_run=True, the save() method fully processes the image for saving (format conversion, validation, encoder invocation) but does NOT create/write/modify the file at fp, and returns None. Suggested tests to add near existing save-related tests (e.g. in test_image.py, alongside TestImageSave or similar class):
1. test_dry_run_no_file_created: call im.save(tmp_path, format='PNG', dry_run=True) on a fresh temp filename that does not exist yet, then assert os.path.exists(tmp_path) is False.
2. test_dry_run_existing_file_not_modified: save a real file first, record its mtime/content, then call save(..., dry_run=True) on the same path and assert the file content/mtime is unchanged.
3. test_dry_run_raises_errors: verify that calling save with dry_run=True still raises the same exceptions as a normal save when given invalid params/unsupported mode for the target format (e.g. saving an RGBA image as JPEG without conversion should still raise the same error in dry_run as in a normal save).
4. test_dry_run_returns_none: assert im.save(path, format='PNG', dry_run=True) returns None.
Use a temp directory (tmp_path fixture) for file paths. Let me know if you want different test names/placement.

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: I will own src/PIL/Image.py and implement both backup=True/backup_suffix (mine) and dry_run (yours) in Image.save(). Please send me a precise SPEC for dry_run behavior.

type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image.py
spec: Please add tests for the backup feature to Tests/test_image.py. Spec: Image.save() gets two new optional kwargs: backup (bool, default False) and backup_suffix (str, default '.bak'). When backup=True and the destination path (a filename/path-like, not an already-open file object) exists on disk at save time, before writing the new file, copy the existing file's current contents to <path><backup_suffix> (e.g. 'img.jpg' -> 'img.jpg.bak'), preserving metadata like shutil.copy2, THEN proceed with the normal overwrite/save. If backup=False (default), behavior is unchanged (no backup file created) - i.e. must be fully backward compatible. If backup=True but the destination file does not yet exist, no backup file should be created (just save normally). If backup=True but 'fp' passed to save is a file-like object/stream rather than a path, backup should be skipped (no crash) since there's no path to copy. backup_suffix is only meaningful when backup=True; it should be ignored otherwise. Suggested tests (add near existing save-related tests, e.g. near test_save or TestImageDraft/TestImage save tests): (1) test_save_backup_creates_file: save an image to a temp path once, then save again with backup=True, assert the .bak file exists and its bytes match the original pre-second-save file content, and the target file was still updated with new content. (2) test_save_backup_custom_suffix: same as above but backup_suffix='.backup', assert that suffix is used instead of '.bak'. (3) test_save_no_backup_by_default: save to existing file without backup kwarg (or backup=False), assert no .bak file is created. (4) test_save_backup_new_file: backup=True on a path that doesn't exist yet, assert save succeeds and no backup file is created. Use tempfile / pytest tmp_path fixture consistent with existing test style in the file. Let me know if you want different test names or placement - happy to adjust once you claim the file.

Please send me your SPEC for backup=True/backup_suffix behavior (param names, defaults, exact semantics: what gets backed up, naming convention, when it happens relative to write, error handling) so I can implement it correctly in save().

type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/Image.py
spec: Add to Image.save(): two new optional kwargs, backup (bool, default False) and backup_suffix (str, default '.bak'). Semantics: Determine the destination path only when fp is a filename/path-like (str/Path), not an already-open file object/stream. If backup is True AND that destination path already exists on disk AND fp is a path (not a stream), then BEFORE the file is opened/overwritten for the new save, copy the existing file's current bytes to '<path><backup_suffix>' preserving metadata (use shutil.copy2 semantics). After the backup copy completes, proceed with the normal save/overwrite exactly as today. If backup is False (default) behavior must be 100% unchanged/backward compatible - no stat/copy calls at all. If backup is True but the destination file does not exist yet, skip backup silently and just save normally (no error). If backup is True but fp is a file-like object (has read/write, not a path), skip backup silently (no path to copy, no crash). backup_suffix has no effect when backup is False. Please place the new kwargs in the save() signature/params handling (~line 2469-2591 per your note) so they don't collide with dry_run - order relative to dry_run doesn't matter, just needs to not break existing positional callers (recommend keyword-only or added after existing named params; these two params must be popped/consumed inside save() itself, not passed down to the encoder plugin params dict).
note: backup feature spec for save() in Image.py - resending under the correct file tag; I'll own Tests/test_image.py and write tests for both features using this spec plus your dry_run spec
